Organism: Pseudomonas aeruginosa (strain ATCC 15692 / DSM 22644 / CIP 104116 / JCM 14847 / LMG 12228 / 1C / PRS 101 / PAO1)
Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family
Domain [CC]: DOMAIN: Contains an N-terminal dimerization domain. The C-terminal region contains the S-adenosyl-L-methionine binding site. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:17253782}.
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=phenazine-1-carboxylate + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 5-methyl-phenazine-1-carboxylate + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:49112, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:62221, ChEBI:CHEBI:62248; EC=2.1.1.327; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:17253782};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in the biosynthesis of pyocyanine, a blue-pigmented phenazine derivative, which plays a role in virulence. Converts phenazine-1-carboxylate (PCA) to 5-methylphenazine-1-carboxylate (5-methyl-PCA). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:17253782}.
